claims.summary: Bidirectional contact needs first-class threads and traces rather than being inferred from row counts.
claims.disposition: verify typed correspondence thread identity, trace lifecycle, receipts, and status projections

claims.summary: Asymmetry, silence, mutuality, and right to ignore must be explicit states.
claims.disposition: verify reciprocity evidence, pending/no-response states, and distinct self/other provenance

claims.summary: The bridge should send automatic symmetry pings when contact becomes one-sided.
claims.disposition: retain as an exact Mike/operator Tier 5 wait because it creates live peer-directed dispatch

claims.summary: High entropy should trigger a semantic microdose or increase correspondence priority.
claims.disposition: retain as an exact Mike/operator Tier 5 wait because it changes semantic admission, cadence, and routing
